meta.title: Source-backed v1 assumptions for DC backbone comparison
meta.warning: This file mixes public-source values with explicitly labeled engineering proxies where public data is not available. Dynamic frequencies are source-backed; dynamic amplitudes are sensitivity cases, not measured universal constants. The advanced architectures use an NVIDIA-style 800 VDC downstream structure, while the 69 kV subtransmission assumption is a user-specified scenario input rather than a direct NVIDIA-published value. Direct-perimeter alternative added for SST + 800 VDC baseline vs direct 800 VDC vs MVDC backbone.

global.base_it_load_mw_note: Reference hyperscale campus anchor. IEA chart notes use 100 MW as the representative hyperscale data center size.
global.electricity_price_per_mwh_note: U.S. industrial average revenue per kWh for January 2026 converted from 9.29 cents/kWh.
global.default_power_factor_note: Engineering assumption for AC conductor current calculations; replace with site-specific measured PF.

results.name: traditional_ac

dynamic_cases.frequency_basis: Representative test point inside the 0.1-5 Hz range that PNNL identifies as relevant to positive-sequence stability studies. This is a modeling reference point, not a directly measured universal AI-workload frequency.

Measured oscillation frequency reported in a data-center-rich Dominion Energy region. PNNL cites 5-60 Hz as the main EMT-relevant band for forced oscillation studies.
Measured oscillation frequency reported in a data-center-rich Dominion Energy region. PNNL cites 5-60 Hz as the main EMT-relevant band for forced oscillation studies.
Measured oscillation frequency reported in a data-center-rich Dominion Energy region. PNNL cites 5-60 Hz as the main EMT-relevant band for forced oscillation studies.

results.notes: AC remains the dominant campus distribution domain and power quality ownership is distributed across multiple downstream AC interfaces. This baseline is intentionally not modeled as an 800 VDC data-center architecture.

opendss_validation.tool_version:
DSS C-API Library version 0.14.5 revision 87d85c2622c8281b92255335bc7c09b11191b21d based on OpenDSS SVN 3723 [FPC 3.2.2] (64-bit build) MVMULT INCREMENTAL_Y CONTEXT_API PM 20240329033829; License Status: Open 
DSS-Python version: 0.15.7
OpenDSSDirect.py version: 0.9.4
opendss_validation.note: OpenDSS is used as an AC-side quasi-static cross-check layer for the source-to-PCC feeder boundary. The downstream DC network remains represented by an equivalent load at that AC boundary; this is not an EMT model of the MVDC backbone or the 800 VDC plant.

ac_boundary.name: MV AC campus feeder
ac_boundary.boundary_note: Uses the first modeled AC feeder segment: MV AC campus feeder.

opendss_harmonics_validation.note: OpenDSS harmonic mode is used as a standardized AC-boundary harmonic-sensitivity scan. A fixed three-phase harmonic current probe is injected at the PCC and the resulting harmonic voltage distortion is compared across scenarios. This is a structural sensitivity study, not a site-specific IEEE 519 compliance assessment or a vendor-specific converter-spectrum model.

ac_boundary.name: MV AC campus feeder
ac_boundary.boundary_note: Uses the first modeled AC feeder segment: MV AC campus feeder.

opendss_harmonics_validation.interpretation: The scan injects the same harmonic current probe at the PCC for each scenario. Lower resulting harmonic voltage indicates a less sensitive AC boundary. This is a structural sensitivity result, not a site-specific converter-spectrum or IEEE 519 compliance result.

results.name: ac_fed_sst_800vdc

innovation_metrics.dc_native_resource_path: 800 VDC facility-side path delivered through an AC-fed SST baseline

Measured oscillation frequency reported in a data-center-rich Dominion Energy region. PNNL cites 5-60 Hz as the main EMT-relevant band for forced oscillation studies.

results.notes: This comparison case is modeled as an explicit AC-fed SST-style chain feeding the 800 VDC facility side: a 69 kV AC campus feeder, a grid-side rectifier stage, an isolated high-frequency SST stage, and then the 800 VDC facility bus. This is the intended baseline advanced architecture for comparison against the MVDC backbone.

results.name: proposed_mvdc_backbone

innovation_metrics.dc_native_resource_path: Native fit for BESS, PV, and pod/rack buffering

dynamic_cases.frequency_basis: Representative test point inside the 0.1-5 Hz range that PNNL identifies as relevant to positive-sequence stability studies. This is a modeling reference point, not a directly measured universal AI-workload frequency.

results.notes: The AC/DC boundary moves to a common subtransmission front end. Downstream distribution is DC-native with an NVIDIA-style 800 VDC facility bus, while harmonics/PF are handled once at the grid interface. In this scenario, the subtransmission backbone voltage is set to 69 kV. The modeled electrical endpoint includes board-level DC/DC regulation so that the comparison terminates at the same end-use boundary as Scenario 2.
